step_by_step_thinking:
I **agree**, adding another key similarity between them - neither contain polyacrylates directly. Polycarboxylates use acrylic resin components indirectly via powder particles mixed with water, whereas zinc eugenols don't involve acryllic substances explicitly. Nonetheless, both achieve adhesion through calcium phosphate formation in zinc eugenols and silicate leaching in polycarboxylates.
